subject: Ecology
title: Wading Birds
description: A quick, practical guide to Wading Birds.

hook.text: Herons stand still as statues in shallow water, then strike with lightning speed.

buildup.text: Wading birds—herons, egrets, ibises, storks—are the top predators of wetland shallows.

discovery.text: They need specific water depths: too deep and they can't wade, too shallow and fish flee.

twist.text: Water management that changes depth by even 10 centimeters can ruin their entire breeding season.

climax.text: Everglades wading bird colonies crashed when water was diverted—restoration is slowly bringing them back.

punchline.text: For wading birds, centimeters of water mean everything.

quiz.question: Why are water levels critical for wading birds?
options: They need specific shallow depths to hunt
They can swim in any depth
They nest only in deep water
